name: Benalup-Casas Viejas and Vejer de la Frontera Loop
name_original: Ruta circular por La Janda desde Benalup - Casas Viejas
description: Explore the La Janda region of Andalusia on this moderate 28.1-mile (45.2 km) road cycling route from Benalup - Casas Viejas.
region_intro: Road cycling around Benalup-Casas Viejas offers diverse landscapes within the province of Cádiz, Andalusia. The region features low hills with cultivated land and scrubland to the west, transitioning to mountainous areas within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park to the east. Cyclists can explore well-paved routes through the La Janda region, encountering varied terrain and elevation gains. The area is also traversed by the Barbate and Celemín rivers, with the Celemín Reservoir providing a natural setting for outdoor activities. Best road cycling routes around Benalup-Casas Viejas The most popular road cycling route is Benalup-Casas Viejas and Vejer de la Frontera Loop, a 28.1 miles (45.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route features elevation gains and passes through the La Janda region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Los Naveros – Los Naveros loop from Benalup-Casas Viejas, a moderate 33.9 miles (54.6 km) path. This route allows cyclists to explore the diverse landscapes surrounding Benalup-Casas Viejas. Local road cyclists also love the Descent to Barbate – Los Caños de Meca loop from Benalup-Casas Viejas, a 51.9 miles (83.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the coast, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Benalup-Casas Viejas is defined by low hills, cultivated land, and the expansive Los Alcornocales Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult challenges. The routes in Benalup-Casas Viejas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Nearly 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Benalup-Casas Viejas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Benalup-Casas Viejas?
A: There are 14 road cycling routes around Benalup-Casas Viejas, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These include 11 moderate routes and 3 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Benalup-Casas Viejas?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from low hills with cultivated land and scrubland to the west, to more mountainous areas within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park to the east. You'll find well-paved surfaces throughout the La Janda region, with routes featuring varied elevation gains.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult routes available. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10283348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Galiz – Puerto de las Asomadillas loop from Benalup-Casas Viejas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 152 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy views of the Los Alcornocales Natural Park, often called 'the last virgin forest of the Mediterranean'. Routes also pass by the Barbate and Celemín rivers, and the Celemín Reservoir area offers pleasant natural settings. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/232979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Corridor of the Two Bays&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975031829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benalup-Casas Viejas and Vejer de la Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.2 km ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Naveros – Los Naveros loop from Benalup-Casas Viejas&lt;/a&gt;, covering 54.6 km.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Benalup-Casas Viejas?
A: The road cycling routes in Benalup-Casas Viejas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. Nearly 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views or lead towards the sea?
A: Yes, some routes extend towards the coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40378651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Barbate – Los Caños de Meca loop from Benalup-Casas Viejas&lt;/a&gt; is a 83.4 km trail that leads through varied terrain towards the coast, offering different scenery.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or historical sites accessible by road bike?
A: While cycling, you can pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbate Reservoir Dam Path&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3431205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celemín Footbridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has historical depth, with remnants like the Torre de la Morita and the Tajo de las Figuras site known for prehistoric cave paintings, though direct road access to all may vary.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Benalup-Casas Viejas?
A: Benalup-Casas Viejas offers an appealing environment for road cycling throughout much of the year, thanks to its southern Andalusian location. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak summer heat.

Q: Are there any facilities for cyclists in the area, such as bike repair or storage?
A: The region is becoming increasingly cyclist-friendly. The Fairplay Golf & Spa Resort, for instance, highlights Benalup-Casas Viejas as an appealing spot for cycling and offers amenities like jet wash, storage, and repair rooms for cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that feature unique natural formations like wind turbines?
A: Yes, you can explore routes that pass by modern landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benalup - Casas Viejas – Los Zorreras Wind Turbines loop from Las Lagunetas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 84.1 km route that offers views of the wind turbines.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes here?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975031829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benalup-Casas Viejas and Vejer de la Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can have around 300-400 meters of ascent. More difficult routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10283348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Galiz – Puerto de las Asomadillas loop&lt;/a&gt;, can feature over 2,000 meters of climbing.
tips: A town that became famous for the events of Casas Viejas - the anarchist insurrection of 1933. To learn more, it is worth going through the Memorial Space on Calle Nueva 30

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route begins and ends in Benalup-Casas Viejas. As a town, Benalup-Casas Viejas offers various parking options, including street parking, making it convenient to start your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on well-paved surfaces, which is ideal for road cyclists. However, be prepared for the possibility of encountering some short unpaved segments that might be more challenging. Overall, it's a moderate ride with around 310 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the attractive landscapes of southern Andalusia, featuring typical 'Pueblos Blancos' and characteristic cork oak forests. A notable highlight is the View of Vejer de la Frontera on the hill. The route also passes near Benalup - Casas Viejas, which has historical sites like the Torre de Ben-a-Alup.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The La Janda area has historical ornithological importance, so birdwatching can be a highlight. The flora along the paths can include mastics, fan palms, wild olive trees, kermes oaks, and black pines, especially as the route is near the Parque de Los Alcornocales.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region benefits from a mild climate, making it ideal for cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for a comfortable ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads and through towns. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not specifically designed as a dog-friendly trail. If you bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, on a leash, and consider its comfort and safety around traffic.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within Parque Natural Los Alcornocales?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. While it is located near the Parque Natural Los Alcornocales, the route itself uses public roads and paths that do not typically require special access permits for cycling.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 51 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires a decent fitness level due to its distance and elevation gain. While not overly strenuous, beginners might find the 45.2 kilometers and 310 meters of climbing a good challenge. It's not recommended for absolute beginners without some prior road cycling experience.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official paths for short segments, including the Camino de Santiago del Estrecho, Ruta el Abejaruco, and the Corredor Verde Dos Bahías, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Benalup-Casas Viejas, as the starting and ending point, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Along the quiet country roads, you might find smaller villages or establishments, but it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.

waypoints.name: View of Vejer de la Frontera on the hill
